characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Blinded by the Light: Courtesy of Matt's Ultra Flash mask. Flight: Spectrum allows Matt to glide through the air for a short amount of time. The Leader: Matt is the one leading the MASK Team against whatever evil scheme VENOM hatches. As the theme song says: "Trakker is gonna lead the mission" Meaningful Name: It makes sense that a mask with so many functions is called "Spectrum", doesn't it? New Powers as the Plot Demands: Trakker's Spectrum Mask. While most of the other masks only have one power, his can be used to grant super vision, glide, fire laser blasts, or broadcast across a range of frequencies.

characters.name: Matt "Hunter" Trakker
characters.description: Blinded by the Light: Courtesy of Matt's Ultra Flash mask. Flight: Spectrum allows Matt to glide through the air for a short amount of time. The Leader: Matt is the one leading the MASK Team against whatever evil scheme VENOM hatches. As the theme song says: "Trakker is gonna lead the mission" Meaningful Name: It makes sense that a mask with so many functions is called "Spectrum", doesn't it? New Powers as the Plot Demands: Trakker's Spectrum Mask. While most of the other masks only have one power, his can be used to grant super vision, glide, fire laser blasts, or broadcast across a range of frequencies.

characters.name: Scott Trakker and T-Bob
characters.description: Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: T-Bob is normally portrayed as a cowardly and snarky robot buddy to Scott. When push comes to shove, however, he is always ready to assist MASK in their fight against VENOM, even being instrumental in solving some of their missions. Deadpan Snarker: T-Bob resorts to this whenever Scott gets both of them in trouble. Exact Words: Scott really likes to employ this technique when convincing T-Bob to get involved in the MASK mission. Whenever T-Bob repeats the instructions Matt left for them, Scott interprets them in such a way that they aren't technically breaking the rules. Here is an example fromCaesar's SwordT-Bob:When your dad dropped us off, he didn't want us digging around for bronze coins.Scott:I know, but we are looking for gold coins! Free-Range Children: Subverted. Matt actually does not like this trope and tells Scott and T-Bob to stay put whenever he goes on a mission. However, he always leaves the pair without supervision, allowing them to go wherever they please. Happily Adopted: Scott has a very good relationship with his adoptive father Matt.

characters.name: Bruce "Magic" Sato
characters.description: Asian and Nerdy: Is frequently seen working on advanced technology when being called for a MASK mission. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Stops appearing in the Racing Series without any explanation. The Engineer: Mechanic subtype. Bruce sometimes is called on to fix their vehicles in the field. Kōan: Frequently utters these whenever the team stumbles on a mystery. A running gag is that whenever he does so, one of the other MASK agents will express confusion, followed by Matt or Alex immediately figuring out what Bruce means. Mind Over Matter: His Liftor Mask allows him to lift objects without touching them. He is even able to lift entire vehicles with his Mask.

characters.name: Alex "Megabyte" Sector
characters.description: Flight: His Jackrabbit Mask allows him to fly. Omnidisciplinary Scientist: The MASK computer even states his "broad base of scientific knowledge" as an asset. The Smart Guy: As mentioned above, he has knowledge about many subjects, which is invaluable in several missions.

characters.name: Dusty "Powderkeg" Hayes
characters.description: Kinetic Weapons Are Just Better: His Backlash mask fires force beams that knock objects down instead of vaporizing them. Southern-Fried Private: Has the brashness and the accent, but he is very friendly and outgoing.

characters.name: Gloria Baker
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: Despite being a regular in the first season, Gloria only makes a very brief appearance in the second season episode "Demolition Duel to the Death", helping Buddy Hawkes load up his Wildcat equipment, with Hawkes saying he'll see her back at M.A.S.K headquarters. She was never seen again. New Powers as the Plot Demands: Similar to Matt, Gloria's Aura mask has multiple functions depending on what is needed for the story. The Smurfette Principle: She is the only female member of MASK. Official Couple: Shown to be close to Matt Trakker in the first season. By the time of the racing series, they appear to have broken up, and Gloria instead has a keen interest in Buddy 'Clutch' Hawkes. Toyless Toyline Character: Unlike the other members of MASK, there was never a Shark toy, although there was a Gloria action figure, but this wasn't until very late in the toyline. In fact, it was one of the final releases in the Split Seconds line, which was released long after the series was off the air.

characters.name: Brad "Chopper" Turner
characters.description: Cool Shades: Never goes anywhere without his pair of black sunglasses. Master of Illusion: His Hocus Pocus Mask allows him to project illusions to confuse his enemies.

characters.name: Hondo "Striker"McLean
characters.description: Badass Teacher: Downplayed. While he is a teacher in his normal life and is certainly a badass on MASK missions, him being a teacher doesn't really amount to anything in terms of character. Energy Weapon: Both of his Masks, which are aptly named Blaster, allow him to fire laser beams.

characters.name: Buddie "Clutch" Hawks
characters.description: Intangible Man: His Penetrator Mask allows him to turn himself and the vehicle he rides in intangible. Master of Disguise: Is able to convincingly disguise himself as Cliff Dagger during the episodePeril in Paris

characters.name: Calhoun "Stonewall" Burns
characters.description: Shrink Ray: His Gulliver Mask allows him to shrink objects. The Mask is named after Gulliver fromGulliver's Travels, who encounters a kingdom of little people.

characters.name: Jacques "Trailblazer"LeFleur
characters.description: Gratuitous French: Since Jacques is from Québec, he naturally has to include French words once in every few lines he speaks. Invisibility Cloak: His Mask, named Maraj, allows him to become invisible. This also extends to whatever vehicle he is driving.

characters.name: Ace "Falcon" Riker
characters.description: Battle Boomerang: His Ricochet mask fires boomerang beams. Vehicular Sabotage: In the racing series, Ace's Cruise Control mask allows him to remotely take over other vehicles.

characters.name: Boris "The Czar" Bushkin
characters.description: Bald Head of Toughness: Is a physically strong guy with a bald head. Husky Russkie: He fits this trope to a T. A big, strong brute whose name is Boris. The Mole: He acts as an antisocial brute to Buddie so VENOM will recruit him. When VENOM assigns him to destroy Buddie, he pretends to do so, but it then turns out he was on MASK's side all along.

characters.name: "Chief" Nevada Rushmore
characters.description: Spontaneous Weapon Creation: His Totem Mask has the ability to materialize small totem poles to throw at his enemies.

characters.name: Ali "Lightning" Bombay
characters.description: Blow You Away: His Vortex Mask allows him to generate whirlwinds.

characters.name: Miles "Wolf" Mayhem
characters.description: Acid Attack: His Mask, Viper, allows him to spit highly corrosive acid. Animal Theme Naming: Both of his masks in the first season are named after snake species, namely Viper and Python. Barrier Warrior: The Flexor Mask Mayhem uses in the Racing Series allows him to create barriers made of energy. Big Bad: He leads VENOM and initiates every evil scheme done by them. Evil Old Folks: Mayhem appears to be in his sixties, judging from his gray hair and wrinkled face.

characters.name: Sly "Wrecker" Rax
characters.description: Cool Shades: Sports a pair of extravagant-looking shades. Deadpan Snarker: Frequently reacts with a snark whenever one of his colleagues says something stupid. Demoted to Extra: Makes a few appearances in the Racing Series, but is no longer one of the main villainous characters. Flechette Storm: The effect of his Stiletto Mask. It allows Rax to create and fire small darts. He upgrades to the Sawblade mask in the racing series, which fires small circular sawblades at his target. Knife Outline: Despite being perfectly able to injure, Rax only uses his Stiletto Mask to pin people to the wall or ground this way.

characters.name: Cliff "Blaster" Dagger
characters.description: Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Like Bruce Sato, Dagger suddenly stops appearing during the Racing Series. Dumb Muscle: Looks to be very physically intimidating, but sounds and acts dimwitted. Eyepatch of Power: Wears an eyepatch over his left eye. Playing with Fire: His Mask, Torch, acts like an easily portable flamethrower.

characters.name: Vanessa Warfield
characters.description: The Baroness: A mild case of the Sexpot variety. Vanessa is an attractive but cold VENOM operative who, as an added bonus, is able to generate whips of energy with her Mask. Dragon Ascendant: The Racing Series has several episodes in which Mayhem is absent, and Vanessa takes over his leading position. Toyless Toyline Character: Narrowly averted. Vanessa and her Manta only received a toy near the end of the animated series, despite being present from the very beginning. Smurfette Principle: Like Gloria in the MASK team, Vanessa is the only female member of VENOM. Come Season 2, Vanessa would be the only major female character in the show due to Gloria beingDemoted to Extra. Whip of Dominance: She can generate energy whips with her Mask, fitting her as aThe Baronesstype villainess with a cold, domineering, and malicious personality who often demeans her colleagues.

characters.name: Bruno "Mad Dog" Sheppard
characters.description: Dumb Muscle: While he isn't as dimwitted as Dagger, Sheppard still fits the archetype of a musclebound but slow-witted henchman. Magnetism Manipulation: His Magna-Beam mask allows him to have telekinetic control over metallic objects. Voice Changeling: Is heard perfectly imitating Brad's voice during the Racing Series.

characters.name: Nash "Goon" Gorey
characters.description: Extreme Doormat: When it comes to Miles Mayhem, Nash Gorey has no spine whatsoever and agrees with every insult Mayhem throws at him. The Friend Nobody Likes: While VENOM agents aren't exactly friendly to each other, Nash gets to endure the most abuse for his boot-licking tendencies. As good as all interactions between him and other agents is him being insulted for being a boot-licker to Mayhem. Nerd Glasses: Wears a pair of oversized glasses befitting his archetype. Super-Strength: His Mask, Powerhouse (sometimes named Samson), grants him enormous physical strength. Yes-Man: This is his only notable character trait. Almost all of his lines are him worshiping Miles Mayhem and agreeing to his plans.

characters.name: Floyd "Birdman" Malloy
characters.description: Anime Hair: Sports a blond gravity-defying hairdo that kind of makes him resemble a Super Saiyan fromDragon Ball Z

characters.name: Lester "The Lizard" Sludge
characters.description: Dishing Out Dirt: Both his Mask and his vehicle can spit out large amounts of mud. Evil Laugh: A very annoying high-pitched one at that.

characters.name: Maximus Mayhem
characters.description: An Ice Person: His mask, Deep Freeze, allows him to generate walls of ice. Cut Lex Luthor a Check: Tells his brother to go on the straight path, as doing so will amass them more money and power compared to constantly acting out evil schemes. Heel–Face Turn: Subverted. Maximus is the one coming up with the idea to turn a new leaf. When Miles Mayhem merely pretends to do so in order to lure MASK into a false sense of security, Max really thought they were going to abandon their evil ways. After Miles reveals his true intentions, Max's anger stems more from the fact that Miles fooled him, rather than him remaining evil. He also quickly offers to assist Miles in his evil plan, showing his intention to turn good wasn't genuine.
